description: A tall slender grass growing in water or wet ground; the hollow stem of marshes. 'Weoli' names what grows where water meets land—the swaying boundary plant.
sound_symbolism: The word waves wet. 'We' sways with wetland movement, reed bending. The vowel 'o' opens hollow inside. Then 'li' flows with slender grace.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'weoli' (the reed). Wetland plant. For baskets, mats, thatching, instruments.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Slender grace—the reed's form inspiring flowing Art Nouveau lines.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Reeds served many needs—thatching, weaving, writing, the versatile wetland harvest.
